title: Use default Trust manager and fallback to user installed CA
author: TimoPtr

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR updates the app’s TLS configuration to use Android’s default trust manager (restoring proper chain building and `network_security_config` behavior) while adding a targeted fallback that trusts only user-installed CAs on ROMs where the default path does not honor them.

**Changes:**
- Switch OkHttp TLS setup back to the platform default trust manager and add a user-installed-CA-only fallback trust manager.
- Introduce `CompositeX509ExtendedTrustManager` to consult a fallback trust manager only when the primary rejects with `CertificateException`.
- Add unit tests for the composite trust manager and for filtering `AndroidCAStore` entries to user-installed CAs; add a StrictMode ignore rule for the keystore disk read during OkHttp configuration.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| common/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/common/data/TLSHelper.kt | Reworks OkHttp TLS setup to use the default trust manager and optionally wrap it with a user-CA-only fallback; adds helpers for keystore loading/filtering. |
| common/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/common/data/CompositeX509ExtendedTrustManager.kt | Adds a composite trust manager that retries server validation against a fallback only after primary `CertificateException`. |
| common/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/common/data/CompositeX509ExtendedTrustManagerTest.kt | Adds unit tests covering fallback decision logic and error propagation/suppression behavior. |
| common/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/common/data/TLSHelperTest.kt | Adds unit tests for filtering user-installed CA entries from a keystore. |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/util/IgnoreViolationRules.kt | Ignores a StrictMode disk-read violation triggered during OkHttp client configuration (keystore access). |
</details>

title: Revert "Preserve WebViewActivity path when switching URLs (#6447)"
author: TimoPtr

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This pull request reverts PR #6447’s WebView path-preservation refactor to reduce risk for an urgent bugfix release (related to #6810), restoring the prior URL-loading and back-navigation behavior in the Android app’s legacy WebView flow.

**Changes:**
- Remove the `Uri.toRelativeUrl()` helper and its unit tests, along with the presenter/view plumbing that used it.
- Revert WebView presenter URL-handling logic to no longer preserve the current path on base URL changes.
- Remove the custom back-navigation resolver (`resolveBackAction` / `BackAction`) and its tests, returning to simpler `WebView.canGoBack()` handling.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| common/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/util/UriExtensionsTest.kt | Removes tests for `toRelativeUrl()` as part of reverting path preservation |
| common/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/util/UrlUtil.kt | Removes `Uri.toRelativeUrl()` extension and its documentation |
| app/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/webview/WebViewPresenterImplTest.kt | Removes tests and fakes tied to “preserve current path on base URL change” behavior |
| app/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/util/compose/webview/WebViewBackNavigationTest.kt | Deletes tests for the custom back action resolver |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/webview/WebViewPresenterImpl.kt | Reverts URL flow handling to not preserve relative path; simplifies history-keeping logic |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/webview/WebViewActivity.kt | Removes custom back resolution and relative-URL support from the activity |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/webview/WebView.kt | Removes `getCurrentWebViewRelativeUrl()` from the `WebView` interface |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/util/compose/webview/BackAction.kt | Deletes the back-navigation resolver implementation (`BackAction` + `resolveBackAction`) |
</details>

title: [NOT_MERGED_GIT_ISSUE] Use default Trust manager and fallback to user installed CA
author: TimoPtr

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR updates the app’s OkHttp TLS configuration to rely on Android’s default trust manager (restoring proper chain building and `network_security_config.xml` behavior) while adding a targeted fallback that can still trust *user-installed* CAs on ROMs that don’t expose them via the default path. This aims to fix SSL handshake failures caused by the prior `AndroidCAStore` snapshot approach (#6810) without regressing custom CA support (#5565).

**Changes:**
- Switch server-certificate validation back to the platform default trust manager and add a user-CA-only fallback trust manager.
- Introduce a composite `X509ExtendedTrustManager` that consults a fallback only when the primary rejects with `CertificateException`.
- Add unit tests for both the composite trust manager behavior and the user-CA filtering logic.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| common/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/common/data/TLSHelper.kt | Reworks OkHttp TLS setup to use the platform trust manager with a user-CA fallback and adds keystore helper functions |
| common/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/common/data/CompositeX509ExtendedTrustManager.kt | Adds a composite trust manager that falls back only on `CertificateException` |
| common/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/common/data/CompositeX509ExtendedTrustManagerTest.kt | Tests fallback decision logic across all `checkServerTrusted` overloads and verifies suppression behavior |
| common/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/common/data/TLSHelperTest.kt | Tests filtering AndroidCAStore entries so only `user:` CAs are included in the fallback keystore |
Tested with incomplete `fullchain.pem` and selfsigned and works as expected now 🥳 . Minor things to check before merging but nothing blocking.

title: Use our configured OkHttpClient for connectivity checks
author: jpelgrom

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR updates the app’s connectivity diagnostics (`DefaultConnectivityChecker`) to perform TLS/server/Home Assistant verification using the app’s configured, mTLS-capable `OkHttpClient` instead of `HttpURLConnection`, aligning connectivity checks with real app networking behavior (resolving #6760).

**Changes:**
- Inject and reuse the configured `OkHttpClient` for TLS, server, and Home Assistant manifest checks
- Mark hostnames/URLs as sensitive in debug logs to avoid leaking user data
- Add a small helper to derive a checker-specific `OkHttpClient` with shorter timeouts
Thanks for hiding the URLs/hostname from the logs.

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR fixes several back-navigation edge cases around the Compose-based `FrontendScreen`, ensuring that system back (including predictive back) behaves correctly while still allowing WebView history back when appropriate. It also clears WebView history after connecting / switching servers so prior pages (e.g., placeholders or previous server content) are not reachable via back.

**Changes:**
- Pop the onboarding destination off the nav back stack when onboarding completes, preventing onboarding from being reachable via back navigation.
- Track WebView back availability via `HAWebViewClient.doUpdateVisitedHistory` and surface it through `FrontendViewState.Content.canGoBack`, using it to conditionally install a `BackHandler` that delegates to `webView.goBack()`.
- Clear WebView history when transitioning from `Loading` → `Content` after a successful frontend connect (including after server switches), and add/adjust unit + UI/screenshot tests accordingly.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 11 out of 11 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/util/compose/HANavHost.kt | Clears onboarding from the navigation back stack when onboarding completes |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/navigation/FrontendNavigation.kt | Stops wiring back presses to `navController.popBackStack` from `FrontendScreen` |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/util/HAWebViewClient.kt | Adds `onCanGoBackChanged` callback and reports `WebView.canGoBack()` on history updates |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/FrontendViewState.kt | Adds `canGoBack` to `FrontendViewState.Content` to drive UI back handling |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/FrontendViewModel.kt | Updates content state with `canGoBack` and emits `ClearHistory` on `Loading` → `Content` connect |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/FrontendScreen.kt | Installs a conditional `BackHandler` to go back in WebView only when it can; removes explicit nav back callback; documents overlay back-handling expectations |
| app/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/util/HAWebViewClientTest.kt | Adds parameterized test validating `onCanGoBackChanged` behavior |
| app/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/FrontendViewModelTest.kt | Updates factory mocks for new parameter and adds back-navigation/clear-history state tests |
| app/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/FrontendScreenTest.kt | Updates composable tests for removed `onBackClick` parameter |
| app/src/screenshotTest/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/FrontendScreenScreenshotTest.kt | Updates screenshot tests for removed `onBackClick` parameter |
| app/src/screenshotTest/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/FrontendScreenImprovScreenshotTest.kt | Updates screenshot test helper for removed `onBackClick` parameter |
</details>
Despite pushing for it on the other PR, I never noticed the back handling didn't work properly here 😳. Thanks for the fix!

title: Implement always open default dashboard in FrontendScreen
author: TimoPtr

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR restores the “always open default dashboard” behavior by resetting the frontend to the server’s default dashboard when the app moves to the background, and centralizes the “navigate to default dashboard” flow in `FrontendViewModel` (including a legacy fallback for pre-2025.6 servers).

**Changes:**
- Add an `onLeavingApp` hook from `FrontendScreen` → `FrontendViewModel` to reset the frontend on app backgrounding (with URL exclusions for HA settings/add-ons areas)
- Simplify gesture handling by introducing `GestureResult.NavigateToDefaultDashboard` and moving version-dependent navigation (external bus vs. legacy sidebar click) into `FrontendViewModel`
- Add/adjust unit tests to cover the new gesture result, leaving-app behavior, and the legacy sidebar navigation action

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/FrontendScreen.kt | Emits `onLeavingApp(webView.url)` on activity `ON_STOP` to trigger first-view reset logic |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/FrontendViewModel.kt | Implements `onLeavingApp` + consolidated “clear history then navigate to default dashboard” with 2025.6+ external-bus path and legacy fallback |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/WebViewAction.kt | Adds deprecated legacy `NavigateToDefaultPanelViaSidebar` action and extracted JS script constant |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/gesture/GestureResult.kt | Replaces chained WebView-action gesture result with a dedicated `NavigateToDefaultDashboard` result |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/gesture/FrontendGestureManager.kt | Returns `NavigateToDefaultDashboard` for the dashboard gesture (defers version handling to the ViewModel) |
| app/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/FrontendViewModelTest.kt | Adds tests for leaving-app behavior and updated gesture navigation behavior across server versions |
| app/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/WebViewActionTest.kt | Adds test coverage for the legacy sidebar navigation WebViewAction |
| app/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/gesture/FrontendGestureManagerTest.kt | Updates gesture test expectations for the new gesture result |
</details>

**Changes:**

- Add a helper (`distanceToZoneCenter`) to compute zone-center distance for tie-breaking
Matches core architecture decision

## Pull request overview

This PR updates the onboarding connection flow to capture the final URL after WebView redirects (notably scheme/port changes such as `:8123 -> :80`) and use that updated origin when emitting the authenticated navigation event, so the app persists the correct base URL for the server once onboarding completes.

title: Refactor Matter/Thread managers to simplify the API and add tests
author: TimoPtr

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR refactors the Matter and Thread manager APIs to remove `Context`-passing from callers by injecting Play Services clients via Hilt (full flavor only), and adds unit tests to cover the new API surface and WebView presenter behavior in the Matter/Thread flow.

**Changes:**
- Replace callback/context-based Matter commissioning with a suspend API returning a sealed `MatterCommissioningResult`.
- Split Thread credential handling into an explicit `exportThreadCredentials()` (export-only) plus a simplified `syncPreferredDataset()` (full sync).
- Add unit tests for MatterManagerImpl, ThreadManagerImpl, and WebViewPresenterImpl’s Matter/Thread state transitions.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 17 out of 17 changed files in this pull request and generated 8 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| app/src/testFull/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/thread/ThreadManagerImplTest.kt | New unit tests for ThreadManagerImpl export/support-gates behavior |
| app/src/testFull/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/matter/MatterManagerImplTest.kt | New unit tests for MatterManagerImpl commissioning/support-gates behavior |
| app/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/webview/WebViewPresenterImplTest.kt | New presenter tests for Matter/Thread step + intent capture |
| app/src/minimal/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/thread/ThreadManagerImpl.kt | Minimal flavor stub updated for new ThreadManager API |
| app/src/minimal/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/matter/MatterManagerImpl.kt | Minimal flavor stub updated for new MatterManager API |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/webview/WebViewPresenterImpl.kt | Presenter updated to use new suspend Matter/Thread APIs |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/webview/WebViewPresenter.kt | Presenter interface updated (removed `Context` params) |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/webview/WebViewActivity.kt | Activity updated to call new presenter methods |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/thread/ThreadManager.kt | ThreadManager API refactor + updated KDoc |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/settings/developer/DeveloperSettingsPresenterImpl.kt | Updated to new `syncPreferredDataset(serverId, scope)` signature |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/matter/MatterManager.kt | MatterManager API refactor + updated KDoc |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/matter/MatterCommissioningResult.kt | New sealed result type for commissioning preparation |
| app/src/full/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/thread/ThreadPlayServicesModule.kt | New Hilt module providing ThreadNetworkClient (full flavor) |
| app/src/full/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/thread/ThreadManagerImpl.kt | ThreadManagerImpl refactor to injected ThreadNetworkClient + new APIs |
| app/src/full/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/matter/MatterPlayServicesModule.kt | New Hilt module providing CommissioningClient + ComponentName (full flavor) |
| app/src/full/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/matter/MatterManagerImpl.kt | MatterManagerImpl refactor to injected commissioning dependencies + suspend API |
| app/src/full/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/matter/MatterCommissioningServiceComponent.kt | New qualifier for injecting the commissioning service ComponentName |
</details>




<details>
<summary>Comments suppressed due to low confidence (2)</summary>

**app/src/full/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/thread/ThreadManagerImpl.kt:224**
* When using `suspendCancellableCoroutine`, callbacks should guard against resuming a cancelled continuation (e.g., `if (cont.isActive) ...`) to avoid `IllegalStateException` if the coroutine is cancelled before Play Services completes.
```
            suspendCancellableCoroutine { cont ->
                threadNetworkClient
                    .addCredentials(threadBorderAgent, threadNetworkCredentials)
                    .addOnSuccessListener { cont.resume(Unit) }
                    .addOnFailureListener { cont.resumeWithException(it) }
            }
```
**app/src/full/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/thread/ThreadManagerImpl.kt:280**
* `suspendCancellableCoroutine` continuations can be cancelled; without guarding `resume(...)` here, callbacks may try to resume a cancelled continuation and throw. Add `cont.isActive` checks.
```
    private suspend fun isPreferredCredentials(credentials: ThreadNetworkCredentials): Boolean =
        suspendCancellableCoroutine { cont ->
            threadNetworkClient
                .isPreferredCredentials(credentials)
                .addOnSuccessListener { cont.resume(it == IsPreferredCredentialsResult.PREFERRED_CREDENTIALS_MATCHED) }
                .addOnFailureListener { cont.resumeWithException(it) }
        }
```
</details>
Thanks for picking this up and improving on my work 🙏. Nice to see how some things can be structured differently yet at the same time no major overhaul like other parts.

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 18 out of 18 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
Tested flows on a real device with Thread credentials syncing and pairing a Matter over Thread device, no issues encountered.

## Pull request overview

Adds WebView URL interception support for the Frontend route so `app://` and `intent:` links can be handled natively, while non–same-origin URLs are routed to the host for external handling (building on #6994’s intent sanitization).

**Changes:**
- Add `Uri.hasSameOrigin(String?)` convenience overload and expand unit tests for origin comparison.
- Introduce new `FrontendEvent` types (`LaunchApp`, `LaunchIntent`) and wire them through `FrontendEventHandler` and navigation.
- Implement URL interception in `FrontendViewModel` and add tests validating emitted events for intercepted URLs.

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| common/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/util/UriExtensionsTest.kt | Adds coverage for the new `hasSameOrigin(String?)` overload and null handling. |
| common/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/util/UrlUtil.kt | Adds `Uri.hasSameOrigin(String?)` overload using string parsing. |
| common/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/common/util/ContextExt.kt | Updates KDoc for `parseExternalIntentUri` with an `@throws` description. |
| app/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/navigation/FrontendEventHandlerTest.kt | Adds tests for new `LaunchApp`/`LaunchIntent` event handling callbacks. |
| app/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/FrontendViewModelTest.kt | Adds tests for URL interception behavior and emitted `FrontendEvent`s. |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/url/FrontendExternalAppLauncher.kt | New helper for launching packages or `intent:` URIs (fallback to store listing). |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/navigation/FrontendNavigation.kt | Wires new events into the host via `launchAppOrStore` / `launchIntentUri`. |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/navigation/FrontendEvent.kt | Adds new event types representing app/intent launches. |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/frontend/FrontendViewModel.kt | Adds interception hook into WebView client and URL routing logic. |
</details>

title: Forbid external URI to use not exported app components
author: TimoPtr

reviews.body:
## Pull request overview

This PR introduces a small sanitization layer for `intent:` URIs coming from untrusted sources (e.g., WebView links and server-driven notifications) to prevent explicit redirection into this app’s non-exported components, and wires that sanitization into the main `intent:` handling entry points.

**Changes:**
- Add `Intent.stripSelfNonExportedTarget(Context)` to strip explicit component/selector targeting of this app’s non-exported activities and report via `FailFast`
- Add `Context.parseExternalIntentUri(String)` as a single-call parse + sanitize helper
- Adopt the new helper in `WebViewActivity` and `MessagingManager`, and add Robolectric coverage for the sanitization behavior

### Reviewed changes

Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

<details>
<summary>Show a summary per file</summary>

| File | Description |
| ---- | ----------- |
| common/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/common/util/IntentExt.kt | Adds intent sanitization to strip explicit targeting of the app’s non-exported activities |
| common/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/common/util/ContextExt.kt | Adds a Context helper to parse + sanitize untrusted `intent:` URIs in one call |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/webview/WebViewActivity.kt | Uses the new parse/sanitize helper when handling `intent:` links from the WebView |
| app/src/main/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/notifications/MessagingManager.kt | Uses the new parse/sanitize helper when handling `intent:` URIs from notifications |
| common/src/test/kotlin/io/homeassistant/companion/android/common/util/IntentExtTest.kt | Adds Robolectric tests covering the new sanitization behavior |
</details>
